things.name: Harbin International Ice and Snow Festival
things.description: January - world's largest ice festival with ice cities, sculptures and coloured lights

things.name: Sun Island Ice Sculpture International Competition
things.description: Thousands of elaborate ice sculptures in a riverside park

things.name: Zhongyang Pedestrian Street (Central Street)
things.description: European Art Nouveau architecture built by Russians in the early 20th century

things.name: Saint Sophia Cathedral
things.description: 1907 Russian Orthodox cathedral with an onion dome - Harbin's most iconic building

things.name: Siberian Tiger Park
things.description: World's largest Siberian tiger breeding centre with 1,000+ tigers

things.name: Zhaolin Park
things.description: The main ice lantern park during the Ice Festival with illuminated sculptures

things.name: Frozen Songhua River Activities
things.description: Ice fishing, ice cycling and horse-drawn sled rides on the frozen river

things.name: Volga Manor
things.description: Replica Russian village with European architecture, museums and restaurants

things.name: Harbin Jewish History Museum
things.description: History of Harbin's once-large Jewish community who arrived via the Trans-Siberian Railway

things.name: Sausage (Ha Hong Red Sausage)
things.description: Harbin's most famous local product - Russian-influenced garlic sausage
